top_categories.workout_context: 150 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic BP MS 150's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
BP MS 150's rolling Texas countryside roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 150 miles.
BP MS 150's rolling Texas countryside roads rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on rolling Texas countryside roads, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
150 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what BP MS 150's rolling Texas countryside roads actually does to you.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 150 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of BP MS 150. Train it until it feels boring.
At BP MS 150, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At BP MS 150, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on rolling Texas countryside roads. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 150 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 150 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at BP MS 150 requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling texas countryside roads to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 150 miles of rolling texas countryside roads in Houston to College Station, Texas, USA.
